_readme: Seed rates for price_book (doc 02 §8). INR, per app/providers/pricing.py's units:
token_in/token_out/char are priced PER 1,000; audio_sec per second; call_min per
These are ESTIMATES converted from vendors' public USD list prices at ~INR 84/USD
and rounded up. They are seeds, not contract rates: the price book is admin-editable
(S18) precisely so real invoice numbers replace these without a deploy, and
usage_events keeps unit_cost_ref so history re-prices correctly when they do.
Rounding is deliberately UP. A cost dashboard that flatters us is worse than
useless — the cost-guard reads these numbers to decide when to downgrade a tier,
and an under-estimate means the budget is already blown by the time it notices.
effective_from is the pilot build date. Do not edit a row in place when a vendor
changes price: add a row with a later effective_from, and history stays true.
Natural key (for idempotent upsert): provider + model + unit + effective_from.

model: "*" = a flat per-provider rate (SMS, telephony, WhatsApp) that needs no per-model row. Not null: the column is NOT NULL, and Postgres treats NULLs as distinct in the unique key, which would let duplicate rates through.

entries.notes: ~$0.30/1M input tokens
~$2.50/1M output tokens
~$0.15/1M input tokens
~$0.60/1M output tokens
STT, ~INR 0.30/audio-minute
TTS billed per character; ~INR 0.15/1000 chars
STT fallback, ~$0.016/audio-minute
TTS fallback, ~$4.00/1M chars
transactional SMS, per message
outbound voice, per minute. NOTE: exotel is also the SMS fallback vendor; if SMS_PROVIDER=exotel, add an exotel/msg row too
WhatsApp. Meta bills per 24h conversation, not per message — this over-counts until S12 adds window tracking
fakes are priced non-zero on purpose: it is what proves the metering path end to end in tests

V-OSS (doc 08): local Qwen on the GPU box. AMORTIZED, not vendor-billed — box capital + power spread over pilot volume, set by you. Non-zero on purpose so the S18 dashboard shows a true V-OSS cost-per-intake instead of a misleading flat zero (doc 08 §3). Wildcard model: any served model prices at the same amortized rate.
V-OSS local LLM output tokens, amortized. See local-vllm token_in note.
V-OSS (doc 08) local Whisper STT, amortized GPU cost per audio second. Wildcard model covers whisper-large-v3-turbo or the S-OSS.1 IndicConformer alternate.
V-OSS (doc 08) local TTS, amortized per 1000 chars. Wildcard model: the bake-off winner (S-OSS.1) prices the same amortized rate whichever engine wins.
V-OSS (doc 08 §1) Voicebox REST — batch V3-pack generation + low-concurrency fallback host, amortized per 1000 chars.
